test_name: Fact Orders has one row per order
rationale: Order grain must be one row per order_id.

test_name: Fact Order Items has unique item keys
rationale: Item grain must be one row per (order_id, order_item_id).

test_name: Fact Orders customer foreign key integrity
rationale: Orders should always map to a customer dimension row.

test_name: Fact Order Items product foreign key integrity
rationale: Order items should always map to a product dimension row.

test_name: All-orders GMV reconciles between item and order facts
rationale: Order-level GMV must reconcile to item-level GMV.
Primary revenue KPI must match whether computed from items or orders.
Primary revenue KPIs cannot be published with zero qualifying orders.

test_name: Primary revenue excludes canceled and unavailable orders
rationale: Canceled/unavailable orders are operational outcomes and should not inflate commercial KPIs.

test_name: Delivered orders have expected on-time logic coverage
rationale: Delivered orders should almost always have enough timestamp data for on-time classification.
AOV must equal revenue-eligible GMV divided by revenue-eligible order count.

test_name: Monthly primary GMV reconciles to headline primary GMV
rationale: Roll-up checks prevent hidden leakage between monthly reporting and headline KPIs.

test_name: Monthly revenue-eligible order count reconciles to headline
rationale: Primary order volume denominator should align across all executive summary layers.

test_name: Monthly and headline AOV align under weighted definition
rationale: Prevents averaging bias from unweighted monthly AOV rollups.

test_name: Cancellation rate remains in expected operating band
rationale: A broad sanity check to catch severe status-mapping regressions.
Most orders should be evaluable for on-time delivery after model logic.

test_name: Late deliveries depress review score (directional test)
rationale: On-time deliveries should show higher review scores than late deliveries.

test_name: Payment value to GMV ratio remains plausible
rationale: Detects major revenue/payment mismatches after transformations.
